title: Project Manifest
description: A minimal, flat project manifest describing what the project is and where it lives.

$schema.description: Schema URL for editor autocompletion and validation. Not part of the project data.

name.description: Reverse-domain project identifier. Globally unique, used as the canonical name across all ecosystems.

version.description: Canonical version. Single source of truth — all ecosystem version files are derived from this value.

category.description: Project classification. Accepts a single value or a list.

license.description: SPDX license expression. Supports dual licensing (e.g., 'MIT OR Apache-2.0').

keywords.description: Discovery tags. Free text, not validated.

labels.description: Process-defined labels. Vocabulary is governed by the process project and validated at compile time.

homepage.description: Project website or documentation portal URL.

upstream.description: Upstream repository URL for forks. Append #branch to track a specific branch (default: main).

process.description: Process projects this project conforms to. Defines governance: entry types, attributes, constraints, and policies. Multiple processes are supported; constraints accumulate (most restrictive wins).

dependencies.description: Projects this project uses. Dependencies participate in the traceability chain: the compiler expects entries to link across the boundary and warns on coverage gaps.

references.description: Registries and external sources this project cites. References are traceability leaves: the compiler resolves links to them but expects no deeper chain.

classification.description: Data classification level (ISO 27001 / government compatible). Ascending sensitivity. Defaults to unclassified.

metadata.description: Freeform key-value block for org-specific information. Available to templates and tools but not validated by the schema.

categoryValue.description: Project category. Values are informative and may be defined by a process project.

name.description: Short display name for this reference.
